title: hedyotis acerosa subsp. tamaulipana
intro: Hedyotis acerosa subsp. tamaulipana, also known as Tamaulipan buttonweed, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Hedyotis acerosa subsp. tamaulipana can be used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
